issue_title: Error 500 on a valid geometry due to a negative centroidCalculator.sumWeight computation
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.18.1

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

21.0.4

### OS Version

MacOS Darwin Kernel Version 24.5.0

### Problem Description

Hi,

I had some trouble to index a document with a Polygon. The Polygon is very small, with a single hole but it's valid according to the libgeos (tested within QGIS and PostGIS).

I got this error:

```
{'type': 'illegal_state_exception', 'reason': 'Negative longs unsupported, use writeLong or writeZLong for negative numbers [-4796333603149578240]'}
```

This is an error which is quite similar to #131606 except that the geometry is valid.

I run some code step by step with the document provided below and I found some stuff.

In the `CentroidCalculator.visit` method, for a Polygon, there are two calls to `visitLinearRing`, one for the ring and the other for each hole. For the following Polygon, the `weight` computed is 0.0 despite the computed weight for the hole is close to 0, but positive. As this is a hole, the `weight` is negative and thus the `sumWeight` is negative, e.g. `0 + (-9.09e-13)`. When the `out.writeVLong(Double.doubleToLongBits(centroidCalculator.sumWeight()));` is executed, the `Double.doubleToLongBits` of a tiny negative number returns a negative integer.

Sorry in advance if my quite tedious explanation is not so clear... ;) 

### Steps to Reproduce

You can find here the mapping and the document with the Polygon which raises this error.

## Mapping

```json
{
    "mappings": {
        "properties": {
            "location": {
                "type": "geo_shape"
            },
            "id": {
                "type": "integer"
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Document

```json
{'location': {'type': 'Polygon',
  'coordinates': [[[144.9738739160867, -37.812764320048906],
    [144.97387390626216, -37.81276430546101],
    [144.97387394861903, -37.81276433502755],
    [144.97387410671985, -37.81276449365169],
    [144.97387431344814, -37.81276470177617],
    [144.97387422754971, -37.8127646444251],
    [144.97387403679602, -37.812764516780305],
    [144.9738739160867, -37.812764320048906]],
   [[144.9738739160867, -37.812764320048906],
    [144.97387396264085, -37.81276437348886],
    [144.97387400134224, -37.81276441404389],
    [144.97387405619358, -37.81276447205519],
    [144.9738739160867, -37.812764320048906]]]},
 'id': 1}
```

### Logs (if relevant)

I have this stacktrace if it's still relevant:

```
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Negative longs unsupported, use writeLong or writeZLong for negative numbers [-4796333603149578240]
at org.elasticsearch.server@8.18.4/org.elasticsearch.common.io.stream.StreamOutput.writeVLong(StreamOutput.java:272)
at org.elasticsearch.server@8.18.4/org.elasticsearch.lucene.spatial.GeometryDocValueWriter.write(GeometryDocValueWriter.java:35)
at org.elasticsearch.server@8.18.4/org.elasticsearch.lucene.spatial.BinaryShapeDocValuesField.binaryValue(BinaryShapeDocValuesField.java:43)
at org.apache.lucene.core@9.12.1/org.apache.lucene.index.IndexingChain.indexDocValue(IndexingChain.java:995)
at org.apache.lucene.core@9.12.1/org.apache.lucene.index.IndexingChain.processField(IndexingChain.java:763)
```
commit_message:
CentroidCalculator does not return negative summation weights (#135176)

Small polygons with holes can compute negative weights due to rounding errors and break 
serialization for those geometries.

commit_message:
Fix off by one in ValuesBytesRefAggregator (#132032)

There are two bugs introduced in #130510 and #131390 affecting the 
VALUES aggregator. The random tests do not cover these edge cases:

1. The check should be firstValues.size() <= group instead of
firstValues.size() < group when reading values from the firstValues
array. We need to inject nulls with repeated values (to simulate
ordinals) to trigger this case.

2. We incorrectly added positionOffset when reading the group ID. We need
to generate more groups to trigger chunking.

Relates #130510 
Relates #131390

Closes #131878

commit_message:
Fix testCloseOrReallocateDuringPartialSnapshot (#132049)

There's a race between setting a shard as failed
and a concurrent non-partial snapshot, resulting
in a snapshot failure because a primary shard is
not available for backup.

Since the intention of the test is to verify
shard close/failc can proceed while a snapshot
is in progress, there's no need for this extra
randomization.

Closes #131900

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix NPE on empty to_lower/to_upper call (#131917)

Apparently, when calling to_lower or to_upper with no parameters, an NPE was thrown, instead of a proper error. AFAICT, this is an old left-over from when these functions were imported from a much older version.

Resolves #131913.

commit_message:
Clear watch schedules when starting trigger engine (#145325)

When new nodes appear in the cluster the watcher allocation may change
If we don't reset the schedules for a given node, there is a chance
multiple nodes will execute the watch. 

This fix revealed a race condition on watch insertion which has been fixed by 
adding recentlyAddedSchedules collection that handles schedules added 
during the watcher service reload.

issue_title: Indices created with 7.13 cannot be opened with more recent versions when using index sorting on date_nanos
commit_message:
Fix default missing index sort value of data_nanos pre 7.14 (#132162)

This commit fixes an issue whereby indices created prior to 7.14 that have an index sort on a date_nanos field can no longer be opened (with versions >= 7.14). When opening an index the configured index sort, derived from the index settings, must match exactly that of the sort encoded in the index itself. A change to fix a bug back in 7.14 changed this for date_nanos fields whose index.sort.missing value is absent, see #74760. Specifically, the default minimum value changed from Long.MIN_VALUE to 0L.

The change in this commit restores the default minimum value for indices prior to 7.14.

issue_title: ES|QL: automatic prefiltering for semantic_text match queries
issue_body:
tracked in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/123043


When using match with semantic_text fields that are backed by dense_vector embeddings, we should automatically apply pre-filters similarly to what we do for knn https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/131004.

The fact that we don't apply pre-filters right now constitutes a bug.
commit_message:
Auto prefiltering for queries on dense semantic_text fields (#138989)

`knn` queries allow specifying `filters` that will be applied before
the knn search. This `pre-filtering` allows the `knn` to return `k`
results. If such filters are to applied only after the `knn` executes,
then the `knn` returns the `k` matching results but the filters can
filter out some of them thus potentially returning fewer than `k` results.

`semantic_text` fields can be queried with:

DSL

- `match` queries
- `semantic` queries
- `knn` queries

ES|QL

- `match` queries
- `knn` queries

For DSL, `knn` queries allow users to specify direct prefilters.
However, `match` and `semantic` queries provide no way to do so.
Same goes for ES|QL `match`. Noting that ES|QL `KNN` already implements
auto pre-filtering where conjunctions are pushed down to the `knn` query
as prefilters.

This commit implements semantic_text auto pre-filtering for `semantic_text` queries
in DSL (`match` and `semantic` queries) and ES|QL (`MATCH`).

We achieve this by adding an `AutoPrefilteringScope` object to the
`SearchExecutionContext`. When we convert a `bool` query to a lucene query,
we push its `must`, `filter`, and `must_not` clauses to the `AutoPrefilteringScope`.
At that stage queries have already been rewritten. Semantic queries using
`text_embedding` inference endpoints are rewritten to knn vector queries
that are auto-prefiltering enabled. Then, when an auto-prefiltering
enabled knn vector query is converted to its lucene equivalent, we
fetch the prefilters from the `SearchExecutionContext` and we apply
them to the knn vector query - which supports pre-filtering already.

ES|QL queries that contain `MATCH` automatically benefit from this implementation because
they are rewritten in `bool` queries.

Limitations

DSL

- nested queries are excluded from pre-filtering (#138184)

ES|QL

- filters that are not translatable to lucene queries will be applied as post-filters

Relates #132068

commit_message:
[Test] Put shutdown marker on the last upgraded node only (#132157)

This is the same failure as observed in #129644 for which the original
fix #129680 did not really work. It did not work because the the
ordering of checks. The shutdown marker is removed after the cluster
passes ready check so that new shards can be allocated. But the cluster
cannot pass the ready check before the shards are allocated. Hence the
circular dependency. In hindsight, there is no need to put shutdown
record for all nodes. It is only needed on the node that upgrades the
last to prevent snapshot from completion during the upgrade process.
This PR does that which ensures there are always 2 nodes for hosting new
shards.

Resolves: #132135 Resolves: #132136 Resolves: #132137

commit_message:
[Test] Put shutdown marker on the last upgraded node only (#132157)

This is the same failure as observed in #129644 for which the original
fix #129680 did not really work. It did not work because the the
ordering of checks. The shutdown marker is removed after the cluster
passes ready check so that new shards can be allocated. But the cluster
cannot pass the ready check before the shards are allocated. Hence the
circular dependency. In hindsight, there is no need to put shutdown
record for all nodes. It is only needed on the node that upgrades the
last to prevent snapshot from completion during the upgrade process.
This PR does that which ensures there are always 2 nodes for hosting new
shards.

Resolves: #132135 Resolves: #132136 Resolves: #132137
